differentials.name: Acute / chronic cor pulmonale (b)  pericardial abnormality:
differentials.raw: Acute / chronic cor pulmonale (b)  pericardial abnormality:

context: Right Atrial Enlargement Cause:    tricuspid stenosis / regurgitation, ASD, atrial fibrillation, dilated cardiomyopathy, Ebstein anomaly, pulmonary atresia ◊  RA enlarges in rightward + posterior direction PA CXR: √  prominent round superior border at junction with SVC √  > 5.5 cm from midline to most lateral RA margin √  > 2.5 cm from right vertebral margin √  > 50% vertical height of RA compared with cardiovascular mediastinal height (from top of aortic arch to base of heart) LAT CXR: √  sharp horizontal interface with lung above RV √  displacement of heart posterior to IVC mimicking LV enla
